# Software Factory for VS Code 2.9

Release 2.9 is the last release that does not use local AI. It consolidates release metadata and operator-facing guardrails in preparation for the next major migration that will enable local AI execution inside the Software Factory runtime.

## Highlights

- Mark this release as the final non-local-AI baseline; subsequent releases will introduce local AI integration and new runtime contracts.
- Update release metadata and manifests to reflect the new version.
- Documentation and changelog updates to help operators upgrade safely.

## Included notes

- Housekeeping updates to make the upgrade path to local AI clear and repeatable.
- Release metadata (`VERSION`, `manifests/release-manifest.json`) updated to point at `2.9`.
- `README.md` updated to advertise `2.9` as the current release and link to this notes file.

## Validation

Verified with (operator-local checks expected):

- `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/local_ci_parity.py --level focused-local --base-rev main` → pass expected
- Post-release surface consistency: `VERSION`, `README.md` `## Current Release`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `.github/releases/v2.9.md`, and `manifests/release-manifest.json` should all agree on `2.9`.

## Notes for publishing

- Suggested tag: `v2.9`
- Canonical release marker: `VERSION`
- Machine-readable release source of truth: `manifests/release-manifest.json`
- Detailed release history: `CHANGELOG.md`
